spec_match.rationale: The demo now normalizes translateStreaming output so accumulated native chunks and incremental fallback chunks produce the same caption text without duplication.

interactive.rationale: Play, stop, source-language, and target-language controls continue to drive live source and translated caption panes with first-chunk latency feedback.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page still matches the live-captioning use case promised by the feature index and now documents the stream-shape normalization needed for robust output.

edge_cases.rationale: The native/full-so-far and fallback/incremental token stream shapes are both handled by normalizeStreamingChunk before rendering.

references.rationale: The see-also section includes the required ChromeStatus entry and now links directly to the translateStreaming spec anchor.

design_system.rationale: The warning block and labels use shared CSS variables and classes, with no inline styles or amber-on-amber text.

summary: Resolved the streaming-captions critique by normalizing both accumulated native translateStreaming chunks and incremental fallback tokens, rendering partial captions through DOM text nodes, cleaning the warning block contrast, and adding the direct translateStreaming spec reference.
